text.title: Clean Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every arch-pattern-clean application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every import MUST point inward: Domain imports nothing; Application imports only Domain; Infrastructure imports Application and Domain; Presentation imports Application and Domain but NOT Infrastructure.
rule.rationale: Enforcing this mechanically (via import-linter, dependency-cruiser, ArchUnit) prevents the well-known "service calls ORM directly" drift that erases the architecture over time.

rule.statement: Entities MUST encapsulate their own invariants, raise domain events, and expose behaviour methods. Static factory methods (create, reconstitute) replace public constructors so construction always goes through invariant checks.
rule.rationale: The Anaemic Domain Model anti-pattern puts all logic in services and reduces entities to getters/setters. This defeats the purpose of Clean Architecture: there is no domain to protect.

rule.statement: Source-code dependencies MUST point inward only. A module in ring N MUST NOT import from ring N+1 or beyond.
rule.rationale: Outer rings exist to be swappable; inward imports lock the domain to a specific framework/database.

rule.statement: Outbound dependencies (DB, queue, external API) are expressed as ports (interfaces) in the application or domain layer; concrete implementations live in adapter or infrastructure.

rationale.source: Uncle Bob - Clean Architecture (2012); Cockburn - Hexagonal

rule.rationale: Without ports, swap requires a full rewrite. With ports, swap = new adapter.

rule.statement: An import-direction lint MUST run in CI and block PRs that violate the dependency rule.
rule.rationale: Without lint enforcement the pattern decays in 1-2 quarters.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
